Bump ruff from 0.15.4 to 0.15.5 (#14312) Bumps [ruff](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) from 0.15.4 to 0.15.5.
Release notes

Sourced from ruff's releases.

0.15.5

Release Notes

Released on 2026-03-05.

Preview features

  • Discover Markdown files by default in preview mode (#23434)
  • [perflint] Extend PERF102 to comprehensions and generators (#23473)
  • [refurb] Fix FURB101 and FURB103 false positives when I/O variable is used later (#23542)
  • [ruff] Add fix for none-not-at-end-of-union (RUF036) (#22829)
  • [ruff] Fix false positive for re.split with empty string pattern (RUF055) (#23634)

Bug fixes

  • [fastapi] Handle callable class dependencies with __call__ method (FAST003) (#23553)
  • [pydocstyle] Fix numpy section ordering (D420) (#23685)
  • [pyflakes] Fix false positive for names shadowing re-exports (F811) (#23356)
  • [pyupgrade] Avoid inserting redundant None elements in UP045 (#23459)

Documentation

  • Document extension mapping for Markdown code formatting (#23574)
  • Update default Python version examples (#23605)

Other changes

  • Publish releases to Astral mirror (#23616)

Contributors

  • @​amyreese
  • @​stakeswky
  • @​chirizxc
  • @​anishgirianish
  • @​bxff
  • @​zsol
  • @​charliermarsh
  • @​ntBre
  • @​kar-ganap

Install prebuilt binaries via shell script

curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -LsSf
https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff/releases/download/0.15.5/ruff-installer.sh
| sh

Install prebuilt binaries via powershell script

feat: Fix PDF signature positioning accuracy and add tkinter GUI

- Fix page rotation bug: positions now match visual corners for PDFs
  with /Rotate property (90, 180, 270 CW) using inverse canvas transforms

  file pickers, position buttons, sliders, live preview, and log output

  directional arrows (↖ ↗ ↙ ↘)

commit.message:
Add README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GitHub Actions CI workflow and golangci-lint config (#5)

* Add GitHub Actions CI workflow and golangci-lint config

Set up a CI pipeline that runs on pull requests targeting master/main:
- Lint job using golangci-lint v2 with golangci-lint-action v9
- Test job with race detector across Go 1.24 and stable
- Minimal permissions (contents: read) on all jobs

The golangci-lint config enables errcheck, gosec, govet, ineffassign,
staticcheck and unused with test files excluded from linting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Disable persist-credentials on checkout steps

Prevent the GitHub token from being exposed to subsequent steps
since the CI workflow does not need authenticated Git operations
after checkout.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Fix golangci-lint issues: variable shadow and struct field alignment

Rename shadowed err variable in cmd/mount.go and reorder struct fields
in client.go and control.go to minimize GC pointer scanning area.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Fix golangci-lint version format in CI workflow

The golangci-lint-action requires a version in v1.2 or v1.2.3 format,
not just the major version. Use v2.11 to get the latest patch release
in the v2.11.x line.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Quote golangci-lint version string in CI workflow

YAML can interpret unquoted values like v2.11 as a float, causing
the action to receive an invalid version. Quote it to ensure it is
passed as a string.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Remove unnecessary quotes around golangci-lint version

Values prefixed with a letter like v2.11 are already treated as
strings by YAML, so the quotes are not needed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

* Add Go 1.25 to test matrix and CI badge to README

Go 1.25 and 1.26 are the two currently supported Go versions. Add
1.25 to the test matrix alongside 1.24 (project minimum) and stable
(currently 1.26). Also add a CI status badge to the README.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PkRcekSW1que7TkyqCmCuu

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: use hook regenerates full script + elevenlabs + scroll-stopping visual plan

Hook Lab "Use →" now:
- Regenerates the full script (body + CTA) anchored to the selected hook
  using a new generateScriptFromHook service (hook locked, body/CTA rewritten
  to flow naturally from the hook's psychological setup)
- Auto-generates ElevenLabs tagged version in same round-trip
- Shows "Generating..." spinner on the button while in flight
- Closes hook lab and flashes script textarea when complete
- Saves to DB (no manual save needed)

Visual plan b-roll updated:
- Initial bRollIdeas schema description now requires scroll-stopping,
  pattern-interrupt moments (check reveals, crowds, envelope openings,
  raw emotional faces) in both the idea list and scene B-roll
- Applies to every new visual plan generation, not just generateMoreBRoll

chore: ニュースデータを自動更新 (2026-03-11 06:12)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 20 security, performance & reliability improvements across backend+frontend

Security:
- API key lookup uses hash comparison instead of plaintext (main.py)
- _get_real_ip() only trusts proxy headers when client is localhost
- Login lockout uses real client IP, not proxy IP
- Rate limit added on /2fa/confirm-login
- XSS: _esc() applied to domain/risk_level in email templates

Bug fixes:
- Plan 'dev' added to scheduler, scans, admin, payment checks
- Named auditor references replace fragile list indices in scanner.py
- LoginPage uses loginWithToken() instead of double login() call
- AppAuditor: body validation for .env/.git, 302 on /admin not flagged
- lstrip("www.") replaced with proper prefix check
- Dead _counts dict removed from metrics.py
- Hacker scenarios loop rewritten with clean for/else pattern

Performance:
- N+1 query in admin list_users replaced with single subquery JOIN
- SQLite WAL mode + busy_timeout=5000ms

Reliability:
- DB session wrapped in try/finally in PDF generation
- Dashboard useEffect cleanup clears all timeouts
- HTTP connections closed with finally: conn.close()
- resp.read() bounded: 1MB RDAP/HIBP, 2MB crt.sh/CT
- asyncio.get_event_loop() → get_running_loop() across 12+ files

Tests: 1367 passed, 0 failed. TS: 0 errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix focus-steal: restore exact window, increase Electron timing

Remove Electron-specific click-to-focus hack that was aborting the
entire osascript (Python Quartz not available → uncaught do shell script
error → keystrokes never sent). Codex worked fine with the original
activate+paste sequence — the click was unnecessary and destructive.

Restore original timing (0.2s activation, 0.1s paste-to-return) which
was proven working for both Claude and Codex before the focus-steal
changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AdMob management to admin panel + API endpoint

- Admin page at /admin/ads/admob with full config:
  - Global enable/disable toggle + publisher ID
  - Android & iOS ad unit IDs (banner, interstitial, rewarded, native)
  - Frequency rules (interstitial per sessions, cooldown, native per items)
  - COPPA/GDPR compliance settings + content rating
- API endpoint GET /api/v1/config/ads (public, no auth, 30s cache)
  - Platform detection via X-Platform header (ios/android)
  - Fallback to Google test ad IDs if no config
- AdMob link added to admin sidebar navigation
- Config stored in app_settings table as JSON (key: admob_config)

Auto-update aiswitch silently on every run

commit.message:
fix: replace nba_api transport with curl_cffi to bypass stats.nba.com blocking

stats.nba.com uses Cloudflare TLS fingerprinting that blocks Python's requests
library. Switch to direct HTTP calls with curl_cffi (Chrome TLS impersonation)
which successfully bypasses the protection. Also increase timeout to 60s and
add curl_cffi to requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(screenshots): retry once if first attempt fails after 120s

After the initial 60s attempt, if screenshots fail, schedule a retry
120s later. Before retrying, check the public project endpoint to see
if screenshots were already saved (e.g. by a concurrent trigger). Only
retries once. Cleans up both timeouts on unmount.

commit.message:
Don't kill current claude session when switching profiles

pkill -x claude was killing the session running aiswitch, causing
claude to prompt for login on next launch. Now only kills claude/codex
processes from other terminal sessions (different session ID).

commit.message:
ChatPanel AI integration + WebSocket live updates

ChatPanel AI:
- Backend /api/chat endpoint with rule-based intent detection
- 5 intent types: navigate, create, count, aggregate, query, action, help
- Entity matching via aliases + layout definitions
- ERP database queries: counts, aggregates, filtered lists
- Action execution with confirmation flow
- Suggestion chips for quick actions
- Markdown rendering (bold) in chat messages
- 13 tests covering all intent types

WebSocket live updates:
- /ws endpoint via FastAPI WebSocket
- ConnectionManager broadcasts to all connected clients
- action_completed + data_changed events
- Auto-reconnect with 5s backoff + 30s keepalive pings
- Entity list pages auto-refresh on data_changed events
- Skills router broadcasts after every action execution
- Connect on auth, disconnect on logout

Merge develop: add DB timeouts, full weekly ETL, submodule auto-commit

- Add calculate_percentiles.py and calculate_player_stats.py to weekly ETL
  so player stats stay current and %ILE column shows real values in prod

backlog size. App-scoped, with counts and "View" links to fix pages.

commit.message:
Switch production build to webpack for better code splitting

Turbopack preloads dynamic() chunks as <script async> on all routes sharing
the same layout, causing ~160 KiB of unused motion JS on the homepage.
Webpack handles dynamic() correctly, creating truly lazy chunks.
Dev server keeps Turbopack for fast HMR.

fixing the @opentelemetry/api resolution issue where the deploy bundler
looked at /vercel/path0/node_modules/ instead of the subdirectory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Implement SyncMode (ASYNC/SIMPLE/CASCADE) with CascadeErrorMode

Complete implementation of sync mode handling across distributed and
standalone modes with four error handling strategies:

Proto changes:
- Add CascadeErrorMode enum (FAIL_FAST, CONTINUE, COMPENSATE, DEAD_LETTER)
- Add cascade_error_mode to CommandRequest, CascadeError to CommandResponse
- Add sync_mode to SagaHandleRequest
- Add Handle RPC to ProcessManagerCoordinatorService

Service discovery:
- Add saga/PM discovery methods to ServiceDiscovery trait
- Implement K8s discovery via labels (angzarr.io/source-domain, subscriptions)
- Implement static discovery with saga/PM registration

New coordinator services:
- SagaCoordinatorService with gap-filling and sync_mode propagation
- PMCoordinatorService with gap-filling and sync_mode propagation

CASCADE mode in distributed (GrpcAggregateContext):
- call_sync_sagas() for synchronous saga execution
- call_sync_pms() for synchronous PM execution
- Skip bus publishing in CASCADE mode

Standalone router enhancements:
- COMPENSATE mode: Track commands, rollback on error via compensation
- DEAD_LETTER mode: Publish errors to DLQ and continue
- CascadeTracker for command tracking in reverse order
- Tracked variants of sync saga/PM calls

Client updates:
- Python: SyncMode and CascadeErrorMode support in handle_command
- Java/C#/Go/Rust: Consistent sync mode API across all clients

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
